steps.title: List Julius's useful skills
steps.content: The passage opens by saying Julius was very useful, then gives several examples: his knowledge of the neighborhood, roads, watercourses, soils, hunting and fishing places, horses, and dogs. The main purpose is broad, not limited to one skill.

steps.title: Reject narrow readings
steps.content: Choice A invents a comparison between the narrator and his wife. Choice B focuses only on soil, which is just one item in the list. Choice C invents repeated hunting and fishing trips together. The passage explains the different ways Julius was helpful. The answer is D.
